title: rubus thyrsoideus var. gallinimontanus
intro: Rubus thyrsoideus var. gallinimontanus, commonly known as the bramble or thimbleberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family, native to North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that may help to soothe and protect the skin, as well as its potential to support digestive health. The health benefits of Rubus thyrsoideus var. gallinimontanus are thought to include the reduction of inflammation, improvement of cardiovascular health, and support of immune function. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and berries in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
